judge_reason: Descriptions A and B are entirely about different visual content: A describes a New England coastal map with specific island names and a trajectory from East to West, while B describes a different map of a flight path over Sparwood B.C. with different labels, dates, and annotations, indicating they are analyzing two completely different images.
original_image_tags: Hand-drawn map and trajectory diagram on aged paper. The map depicts a coastal area, likely in New England, showing islands labeled "Puffin I.", "Bear I.", "Egg Rock", "Duck I.", "Little Duck I.", "Englishman Bay", and "Bass Harbor Head Light". A trajectory line with arrows indicates an object's path from right to left (East to West). A circle is drawn around "St. Paul Light". A smaller diagram in the lower left shows altitude vs. distance. A compass rose points North to the right. A point is marked "Gerhune residence - Keduck".
gpt_mini_desc: *Image: A hand-drawn map-style diagram on a page stamped “CONFIDENTIAL,” showing a plotted flight path with arrows, labeled land and lake outlines, a circular area around one point, a small boxed data table, an altitude/speed graph, and typed caption text at the bottom identifying it as a photostatic copy of a drawing made by Paul Herring.*
